title: chrysobalanus icaco subsp. atacorensis
intro: Chrysobalanus icaco subsp. atacorensis, also known as West Indian or Cacao, is a subspecies of the Chrysobalanus icaco tree native to tropical regions in the Americas. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chrysobalanus icaco subsp. atacorensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of certain diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, highlighting its versatility and potential in natural health remedies.
